chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Ruling for One Whose Circumambulation is Interrupted by Prayer or Other Matters

text: 2797 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ حَفْصِ بْنِ اَلْبَخْتَرِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِيمَنْ كَانَ يَطُوفُ بِالْبَيْتِ فَيَعْرِضُ لَهُ دُخُولُ اَلْكَعْبَةِ فَدَخَلَهَا قَالَ «يَسْتَقْبِلُ طَوَافَهُ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications: Hadith.2797 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Hafs ibn al-Bukhtari, from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding someone who is performing tawaf around the Kaaba and then enters the Kaaba during the tawaf. Imam (as) said: "He must start his tawaf over from the beginning."
